info.title: Joutes API
info.description: REST API for Joutes, a companion platform for trading card game communities (collection tracking, decks, news, quizzes, scanner/OCR card recognition, events, lairs, leagues, friends, play groups, wishlists and sell lists). Most endpoints require an authenticated session created via the `/auth/*` endpoints (Better Auth). A few write endpoints additionally require a specific permission (e.g. `news:update`, `scanner:ai`) or admin status - this is noted in each operation's description.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication, OAuth2/OIDC provider and session management (Better Auth).

tags.name: Collection
tags.description: Personal card collection, booster/pack tracking and collection completion stats.

tags.name: Decks
tags.description: Public and private deck lists linked to a game.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Tournament / event calendar entries tied to lairs.

tags.name: Friends
tags.description: Friend list, friend requests and friend codes.

tags.name: Games
tags.description: Game catalog, cards, rules, policies, news, quizzes and scanner recognition per game.

tags.name: Lairs
tags.description: Game stores / venues ("lairs") that host events.

tags.name: Leagues
tags.description: League rankings (league CRUD itself is a server action, not a REST endpoint).

tags.name: News
tags.description: News articles, optionally linked to a game.

tags.name: Play Groups
tags.description: Shared groups for pooled collections, wishlists and sell lists.

tags.name: Quizzes
tags.description: Quizzes, optionally linked to a game.

tags.name: Sell Lists
tags.description: Cards a user or play group is offering for sale.

tags.name: Upload
tags.description: Generic admin file upload to blob storage.

tags.name: Wishlists
tags.description: Cards a user or play group is looking for.

tags.name: Cron
tags.description: Internal scheduled jobs, protected by a shared secret. Not intended for client use.

tags.name: Tournaments
tags.description: Standalone tournament management: players, phases, rounds, matches and standings.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Catch-all segment(s) handled by Better Auth, e.g. `sign-in/email-otp`, `sign-out`, `session`, `oauth2/authorize`, `oauth2/token`, `oauth2/register`, `jwks`, passkey and email-OTP routes. The exact set of sub-routes is defined by the Better Auth plugins configured in `lib/auth.ts` (email OTP, passkey, JWT, OAuth provider, Discord social sign-in) and is not exhaustively enumerable here.

get.description: Delegates to Better Auth's internal handler (e.g. session lookup, OAuth2 authorize, JWKS).

200.description: Response shape depends on the Better Auth sub-route invoked.

post.summary: Better Auth catch-all (write operations)
post.description: Delegates to Better Auth's internal handler (e.g. sign-in, sign-out, OAuth2 token exchange, passkey registration).

schema.description: Body shape depends on the Better Auth sub-route invoked.

200.description: Response shape depends on the Better Auth sub-route invoked.

get.description: Framework-generated discovery document for the Better Auth OAuth provider plugin.
Framework-generated OIDC discovery metadata for the Better Auth OAuth provider plugin.

get.summary: Get the caller's collection overview across all games

parameters.name: gameSlug

parameters.name: type

parameters.name: cardId
parameters.description: Catalog card id (not a collection entry id).

patch.summary: Mark/unmark a specific collection entry as borrowed

parameters.description: Specific collection entry id to delete. If omitted, an arbitrary matching copy is removed.

parameters.name: gameSlug
parameters.description: Game slug or Mongo ObjectId.

get.summary: Get the caller's collection completion for a specific game

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on card name.

200.description: Game collection page with completion stats.

get.summary: List all owned printings/variants of a card by exact name

200.description: Array of card variants (raw array, not wrapped).

get.summary: List the caller's boosters/packs for a game

post.summary: Create an empty booster to fill in

delete.summary: Delete a booster and its cards

post.summary: Add a card to a booster

patch.summary: Toggle a booster card's foil status

delete.summary: Remove a card from a booster

post.summary: Copy every card in a booster into the caller's main collection

delete.summary: Remove this booster's cards from the caller's main collection
delete.description: Cascades to remove any sell-list listings referencing the removed collection entries.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Regex match on name/description.

200.description: Paginated decks. Requesting another user's private decks (via `playerId` + `visibility=private`) is rejected.

post.summary: Create a deck

409.description: A deck with this name already exists for this player.

get.description: Private decks are only visible to their owner (session optional; checked if present).

patch.summary: Update a deck, or toggle favorite status
patch.description: Two modes based on body shape: send `{ "favorite": boolean }` to toggle the caller's favorite on the deck, or any subset of the deck fields to update the deck itself (only the owner may do either).

409.description: Name collides with an existing deck of the same player.

get.summary: Get this OpenAPI document as JSON

get.description: Behavior depends on inputs: `lairId` scopes to one lair; an authenticated session returns events for the user's followed games (or `gameId`); an anonymous request with `userLat`/`userLon`/`maxDistance` searches nearby lairs; otherwise an anonymous request with no location returns an empty list.

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Game id, or the special value `followed`.

parameters.name: maxDistance
parameters.description: Max distance in km, must be > 0.

get.summary: List users who favorited an event (organizer/lair-owner only)

post.summary: Send a friend request by username or displayName#discriminator tag

409.description: Already friends, or a request is already pending.

get.summary: Get (or lazily create) the caller's own friend code

post.summary: Add a friend immediately by scanning/entering their friend code
post.description: Unlike `POST /friends`, this adds the friendship immediately (no accept step) and also auto-resolves any pending request between the two users.

409.description: Already friends.

post.summary: Accept a pending friend request (recipient only)

delete.summary: Decline a pending friend request (recipient only)

get.summary: Join a casual game match via an invite link
get.description: Browser-navigation endpoint (not meant to be called via XHR/fetch): always responds with an HTTP redirect, never JSON. Redirects to `/login` if not authenticated, otherwise to the match page with a French success/error message in the query string.

302.description: Redirect to `/login?callbackUrl=...` (not authenticated), or to `/game-matches/{matchId}?message=...` / `/game-matches?error=...` depending on outcome.

get.description: Returns up to 25 games with a lightweight projection; no pagination/filtering supported.

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Game slug or Mongo ObjectId.

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Game slug; used as a Meilisearch index key.

get.description: Response is a bare array unless `page` or `limit` is supplied, in which case it becomes a paginated object including facet lists. `searchQuery` supports inline directives: `set:<code>` / `e:<code>` to filter by set (overrides `setCode`; `set:*` disables set filtering), and `cn:<code>` to filter by exact collector number.

200.description: Cards, or a paginated result if `page`/`limit` is present.

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Game slug (exact match only).

parameters.name: cardId
parameters.description: Card's own `id` field, scoped to the resolved game.

get.summary: Get a card's detail, including its erratas
get.description: Session is optional; if present, `userVote` is populated on each errata.

200.description: Card detail with erratas and mentioned-card lookups.

post.summary: Validate/parse a Riftbound deck list
post.description: Only supported for the game whose slug is `riftbound`. Accepts a Piltover Archive deck URL, a Piltover deck code, or a raw pasted deck list.

get.description: Response body is a raw array; pagination totals are returned via the `x-page`, `x-page-size` and `x-count` response headers instead of the body.

get.summary: Export a game's cards, erratas, policies and rules as a single JSON file
get.description: Generates (or reuses, if one was generated less than 24h ago) a JSON export of the game's cards, erratas, policies and rules (in every available language), uploaded to blob storage. Returns the export's URL rather than the export content itself.

size.description: Size of the export file in bytes.

post.summary: Auto-link recognized card names in free text ("Loupe")

get.summary: List news linked to a game

get.description: Response body is a raw array; pagination totals are returned via the `x-page`, `x-page-size` and `x-count` response headers instead of the body.

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: Full-text search on title/content.

post.summary: (Stub) Create a policy for a game
post.description: Requires permission `policies:update`. Currently a stub: it does not read a request body nor persist anything, and always returns `{}` on success. An unauthorized call surfaces as an unhandled server error rather than a clean 401/403.

get.summary: List quizzes linked to a game

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Game slug (only `riftbound` is currently supported).

get.summary: Get/search Riftbound rules text (Comprehensive Rules / Tournament Rules)
get.description: Rules content is sourced from static local JSON files, not the database.

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: If present, search mode takes priority over document/ruleId listing.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Only used in search mode, defaults to 30.

200.description: Rule entries (shape depends on whether `searchQuery`/`ruleId` is used).

post.summary: Identify a card from a photo using AI vision
Fuzzy-match card text (e.g. from on-device OCR) against the catalog

200.description: Best match, if any (null if text shorter than 3 characters).

get.summary: List distinct card names for a set (used to seed local OCR matching)

parameters.name: gameId
parameters.description: Declared but currently ignored - always returns Riftbound's sets.

get.description: Currently hard-coded to serve the static Riftbound sets data file regardless of the path parameter.
Always anonymous - private lairs are never returned by this endpoint.

Public leagues are visible anonymously. Private leagues require the caller to be a participant or organizer (returns 403, even when not logged in). Note: league search/create/update/delete and match reporting are implemented as Next.js server actions (`app/leagues/actions.ts`), not as REST endpoints, and so are not part of this API surface.

parameters.name: includeTags
parameters.description: Set to `true` to include the distinct list of all tags.

Create a quiz

get.description: Sell lists are always publicly viewable; session is only used to compute `canEdit`.

409.description: Card already for sale.

patch.summary: Update a sell list item's price/currency/note

409.description: Card already for sale.

get.summary: List the caller's own personal wishlists

post.summary: Create a personal wishlist

409.description: A wishlist with this name already exists.

get.description: Session optional; used to compute `canEdit` and view access for non-public wishlists.

409.description: A wishlist with this name already exists for this owner.

delete.summary: Delete a wishlist and all its items

get.description: Session optional; when present, each item is annotated with `ownedQuantity` reflecting how many the current viewer personally owns.

post.summary: Add a card to a wishlist

get.summary: Refresh events for every lair from their event source URLs
Refresh every configured Discord event-board message
Process one pending Discord event-board update from the queue
get.description: Processes at most one event per invocation (`boardsNeedsUpdate: true`); intended to be called repeatedly by the scheduler.

get.summary: List tournaments organized by the current user

post.summary: Create a tournament
Join a tournament by its participation code
post.description: Self-registration via a tournament's join code. With a session, the player is linked to the account; without a session, `displayName` is required and the player is added as a guest and receives their sync key. If pre-registration is enabled, the player is added with status `pre-registered`.

409.description: The tournament is completed (registration closed).

get.summary: List tournaments the current user plays in
get.description: Tournaments where the authenticated user is registered as a player (via their account). No player sync key is required — being logged in is enough.

200.description: Tournaments the user plays in, with their player entry.

get.summary: Get a tournament with its phases and players
get.description: Readable by tournament organizers and registered players.

delete.summary: Delete a tournament and all its sub-resources (organizers only)

post.summary: Register a player by email, username#discriminator, or guest name (organizers only)
post.description:
The `identifier` is resolved server-side:
- `username#discriminator` → the matching user is linked by `userId`; if no user matches, the request fails with 404 (an explicit discriminator targets a specific account).
- an email → the matching user is linked by `userId`; if none exists, a guest account is created for that email before registering the player.
- any other value → a guest player is registered with that display name, without a linked account.

identifier.description: Email, `username#discriminator` tag, or a guest display name.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.

patch.summary: Update a player: rename, reseed, change status
patch.description: Organizers can update any field (rename, reseed, set status registered/dropped). A player themselves (session or player sync key) can only drop out of the tournament, i.e. set their own status to `dropped`.

delete.summary: Remove a player without matches (organizers only)
delete.description: Rejected with 409 if the player already has matches; mark them as `dropped` instead.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.

post.summary: Add a phase (organizers only)
post.description: Crée une phase. `bestOf` définit le nombre de parties du best-of (défaut 1). `resultMode` : selection (désigner le vainqueur) ou points (saisir les points). `scoringMethod` : fixed (points victoire/défaite/nul) ou rank_offset (N + offset selon le rang). `topCut` limite les joueurs qualifiés à l'entrée. bracket est strictement 2-2.

get.summary: Get a phase with its rounds

patch.description: The phase `type` can only be changed while the phase has not started.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.
Conflict with the current tournament state.

get.summary: List the rounds of a phase

post.summary: Create the next round of the phase (organizers only)
post.description: Generates pairings automatically for `swiss` (standings-based) and `bracket` (seeding from the previous phase for round 1, then winners) phases, auto-completing BYE matches. For `freeform` phases an empty round is created and matches are added manually. Fails with 409 while the previous round is not completed.

201.description: Created round with its generated matches.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.

get.summary: Get a round with its matches

delete.summary: Delete the last round of a phase and its matches (organizers only)
delete.description: Only the most recent round of a phase can be deleted, to keep pairings consistent.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.

post.summary: Validate (freeze) or recompute a round's standings (organizers only)
post.description: Computes the phase standings as of this round (cumulative over completed matches up to and including the round) and persists the snapshot on the round, so it is not recomputed on each read. Idempotent: calling again recomputes and replaces the snapshot (the "recompute" button). The round must be completed.

200.description: The round with its frozen standings.

409.description: The round is not completed yet.

get.summary: List the matches of a round

post.summary: Manually add a match to the round (organizers only)
post.description: Intended for `freeform` phases, multiplayer formats or exceptional corrections. Player ids are tournament player ids; a single player creates an auto-completed BYE, 3+ players a multiplayer match.

patch.summary: Operate on a match: report, confirm, dispute or clear a result
patch.description: Discriminé par `action`. `report` renseigne le résultat partie par partie du best-of (`games`) : selection → winnerId par partie (null = nulle) ; points → `points` par joueur et par partie (le vainqueur en est déduit). `confirm` valide un résultat en attente (un organisateur peut valider manuellement) ; `dispute` conteste ; `clear` (organisateur) supprime un résultat rapporté et réinitialise le match. BYE non modifiable.

oneOf.description: Supprime un résultat rapporté (organisateur) : réinitialise le match.

409.description: Conflict with the current tournament state.

delete.description: Only matches of the last round of their phase can be deleted, to keep standings and pairings of later rounds consistent.

409.description: The match is not in the last round of its phase.

get.summary: Get the tournament live state (announcements + timer)
get.description: Public read: broadcast announcements, timer, and the server clock (serverNow) used to synchronise the countdown on clients.

remainingSeconds.description: Temps restant figé lorsque le minuteur est en pause.

post.summary: Publish an announcement (organizers only)
Control the timer (organizers only)
post.description: `start` launches the timer for `durationSeconds` (sets an absolute end instant); `pause` freezes the remaining time; `resume` restarts from the frozen time; `stop` stops it.

remainingSeconds.description: Temps restant figé lorsque le minuteur est en pause.

get.summary: Preview the transition to the next phase (organizers only)
get.description: Returns the current phase (to be closed), the next phase to start, and the entry qualification (players qualified / eliminated by the next phase's top cut).

post.summary: Advance to the next phase (organizers only)
post.description: Closes the current phase, drops (status DROPPED) the players not qualified by the next phase's top cut, sets the tournament's current phase, and creates the first round of the new phase. Returns the created round with its matches.

201.description: The first round of the new phase, with its matches.

409.description: No next phase to start.

get.description: Ordered phases, each with its rounds and match recaps, plus the standings frozen at the end of each round (present once an organizer has validated the round). Accessible to organizers and players.
Computed from completed matches only. Pass `?phaseId=` to restrict standings to a single phase.

post.summary: Resolve player sync keys to their tournaments
post.description: Resolves browser-stored player sync keys (`tpsk_...`) to their tournament and player. The keys themselves act as authentication; unknown keys are silently ignored. Used by the /tournaments page to list synchronized tournaments.

200.description: Resolved entries, in the same order as valid keys.

sessionAuth.name: better-auth.session_token
sessionAuth.description: Session cookie set by Better Auth after signing in via `/auth/*`. Some operations additionally require a specific permission (e.g. `news:update`, `quizzes:update`, `policies:update`, `scanner:ai`) or admin status on top of a valid session - see the operation description.

cronSecret.description: Shared secret for scheduled jobs. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET>`.

apiKeyBearer.description: Personal API key (prefix `jts_`), created from the account page. Send as `Authorization: Bearer jts_...`.

playerSyncKeyBearer.description: Tournament player sync key (prefix `tpsk_`), generated when the player is registered and shared by the organizer (QR code). Grants read access to that player's tournament and match report/confirm/dispute as that player — including for guest players without an account.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not authorized (missing permission/role, or not a member).

ValidationError.description: Invalid request body or query parameters.

details.description: Present on some 400 responses (raw or flattened Zod error).

NestedError.description: Used by a handful of endpoints (games loop/deck-checker/scanner) instead of the flat Error shape.

RuleEntry.description: A rules-document entry (title/keyword/paragraph). `markup` uses a small custom pseudo-markup format (`{{rule id="..."}}`, `{{keyword id="..."}}`, `{{match}}...{{/match}}`), intended for client-side parsing, not HTML.

matched.description: Only present when searchQuery is used.

correctOptionIds.description: Required for single/multiple; at most 1 for single; must reference existing option ids; no duplicates.

gameId.description: 24-char hex ObjectId, or "" to leave unlinked.

ownedQuantity.description: Only populated on the list endpoint, reflecting the current viewer's own collection.

remainingSeconds.description: Temps restant figé lorsque le minuteur est en pause.

TournamentAnnouncementPublic.description: Forme publique minimale d'une annonce, diffusée via /live (sans createdBy ni tournamentId).

userId.description: Linked user account, absent for guest players.

syncKey.description: Player sync secret (prefix `tpsk_`). Only present in responses when the requester is a tournament organizer.

type.description: freeform: pas de génération auto. swiss: rondes suisses appariées au classement. elimination: seuls les vainqueurs avancent, ré-appariés (eliminationSeeding). bracket: arbre d'élimination seedé au classement d'entrée.

bestOf.description: Nombre de parties du best-of (best-of-n). Défaut 1.

resultMode.description: selection: on désigne le vainqueur de chaque partie. points: les joueurs saisissent leurs points, le vainqueur en est déduit.

rankOffsets.description: Utilisé quand scoringMethod = rank_offset : points = N + offset[rang]. Défaut [3,1,-1,-3,-4,-5,-7].

eliminationSeeding.description: Ré-appariement des vainqueurs pour une phase elimination.

topCut.description: Nombre de joueurs qualifiés à l'entrée de la phase.

standings.description: Classement de la phase figé à l'issue de la ronde, persisté lors de la validation par l'organisateur. Absent tant que la ronde n'est pas validée.

standingsValidatedAt.description: Horodatage de la dernière validation (ou recalcul) du classement de la ronde.

winnerIds.description: Empty while no result; on a completed match, empty means a draw between all players, otherwise the (co-)winners.

points.description: Mode points : points par joueur de tournoi.
